Title: BnaPPT1 is essential for chloroplast development and seed oil accumulation in Brassica napus

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plastidic phosphate translocator (pPT) family; phosphoenolpyruvate/phosphate translocator
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed oil bodies; mature seed cotyledon
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Carbon and energy storage in seeds
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of BnaA08.PPT1 together with BnaC08.PPT1 reduces seed oil content and impairs chloroplast development and plant growth
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in root, stem, leaf, and especially developing seed
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in developing seeds; also expressed in vegetative tissues
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout of both BnaA08.PPT1 and BnaC08.PPT1; RT-qPCR; lipidomics; metabolite profiling; TEM; NIRS seed oil measurement
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Growth chamber 16 h light/8 h dark at 22/20°C; field natural conditions
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Lipidomics by LC-MS/MS; primary metabolite profiling by LC-MS/MS
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaA08.PPT1 is one of two redundant BnaPPT1 copies required for normal growth in B. napus. 2. Double mutation with BnaC08.PPT1 caused chlorophyll and plastid membrane lipid reduction, abnormal chloroplast ultrastructure, and lower photosynthesis. 3. Double mutants showed reduced glycolytic intermediates and lower seed oil accumulation.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockout caused severe growth retardation, yellowish leaves, reduced biomass, lower yield, and reduced thousand-seed weight
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Should be maintained functional in breeding programs; combined enhancement with the C-genome copy may support oil improvement while avoiding strong vegetative penalties
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single-copy mutants showed limited seed phenotype, indicating partial functional redundancy with BnaC08.PPT1

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plastidic phosphate translocator (pPT) family; phosphoenolpyruvate/phosphate translocator
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed oil bodies; mature seed cotyledon
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Carbon and energy storage in seeds
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of BnaC08.PPT1 increases seed oil content, while loss together with BnaA08.PPT1 decreases seed oil content and damages chloroplast development
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in root, stem, leaf, and especially developing seed; higher than BnaA08.PPT1 in most tissues
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest in developing seed; also expressed in vegetative tissues
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 35S-driven overexpression; CRISPR/Cas9 double knockout with BnaA08.PPT1; RT-qPCR; subcellular localization with GFP in Arabidopsis protoplasts; LC-MS/MS lipidomics and metabolite profiling; TEM; NIRS; GC-FID
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Growth chamber 16 h light/8 h dark at 22/20°C; field natural conditions
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Lipidomics by LC-MS/MS; primary metabolite profiling by LC-MS/MS
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnaC08.PPT1 encodes a chloroplast membrane-localized PPT1 transporter. 2. Overexpression significantly increased seed oil content by about 2.1–3.3%, while double knockout reduced seed oil by 2.2–9.1%. 3. BnaC08.PPT1 promotes chloroplast membrane lipid synthesis, photosynthesis, seed glycolysis, and neutral lipid accumulation.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression showed reduced seed protein and soluble sugar, with increased starch; no obvious agronomic penalty reported. Knockout caused severe growth and yield defects
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target to raise seed oil content without major agronomic penalties; suitable for constitutive overexpression or allele enhancement while monitoring protein/sugar tradeoffs
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This was the overexpression transgene used for functional gain-of-function analysis and appears to contribute more strongly than the A-genome copy due to higher expression
